entities.name: direct_travel_inc
entities.description: Direct Travel, Inc. is the TMC providing all six core Duty of Care service components defined in Section 3, including itinerary visibility, risk intelligence, pre-trip services, in-trip monitoring, incident response, and post-incident reporting.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will capture and maintain itinerary records for all travel booked through Approved Channels

entities.name: client
entities.description: The corporate client receiving Duty of Care services under Section 3. Client has responsibilities including defining high-risk destinations and approver hierarchies, procuring insurance policies, providing authorization for extraordinary actions, and designating escalation contacts.

entities.name: statement_of_work
entities.description: The SOW governs configuration of multiple service features in Section 3, including: availability of the interactive global map (3.1(b)), pre-trip advisory delivery (3.3(b)), travel insurance verification (3.3(d)), alert channel configuration (3.4(a)), and the extraordinary cost threshold and authorization overrides (3.5(b)).

source_anchor.source_text: Where included in the SOW, Direct Travel will provide access to an interactive global map

entities.name: traveler
entities.description: An authorized individual traveling on Client business. Travelers receive pre-trip advisories, real-time alerts, welfare check outreach during incidents, and rebooking assistance. Travelers remain responsible for contacting local emergency services where there is an immediate threat to life or safety.

source_anchor.source_text: Travelers remain responsible for contacting local emergency services (police, fire, ambulance) where there is an immediate threat to life or safety

entities.name: incident
entities.description: A discrete event that may impact Traveler safety. When an Incident occurs, Direct Travel identifies Travelers in or near the affected impact zone. Incidents classified at Severity Level 2 or above trigger incident response services. Level 4 Incidents automatically trigger post-incident reporting.

source_anchor.source_text: When an Incident occurs, Direct Travel will, using available itinerary data, identify Travelers who are in or near the affected area

entities.name: severity_level_2
entities.description: Severity Level 2 is the minimum threshold at which Direct Travel activates incident response and traveler assistance services under Section 3.5, including welfare checks, rebooking, specialist provider coordination, and client escalation.

source_anchor.source_text: During Incidents classified at Severity Level 2 or above, Direct Travel will provide the following services

entities.name: severity_level_4
entities.description: Level 4 Incidents automatically trigger post-incident summary reporting (Section 3.6(a)) and a post-incident review with Client to identify program improvements and assess response effectiveness (Section 3.6(b)).

source_anchor.source_text: Upon Client request or automatically for Level 4 Incidents, Direct Travel will provide a post-incident summary

entities.name: approved_channels
entities.description: Bookings made through Approved Channels are captured and maintained by Direct Travel, including air, hotel, rail, car rental, and ground transportation segments regardless of airline distribution channel. These bookings provide the itinerary visibility required for duty of care services.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will capture and maintain itinerary records for all travel booked through Approved Channels, including air, hotel, rail, car rental, and ground transportation segments, regardless of airline distribution channel

entities.name: off_channel_booking
entities.description: Travel booked outside Approved Channels. Direct Travel will use commercially reasonable efforts to ingest itinerary data from Off-Channel Bookings where Client has deployed itinerary capture tools (e.g., email parsing, expense integration, or supplier direct-connect feeds). Off-Channel Bookings for which itinerary data has not been provided are not visible to Direct Travel.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will use commercially reasonable efforts to ingest itinerary data from Off-Channel Bookings where Client has deployed itinerary capture tools (e.g., email parsing, expense integration, or supplier direct-connect feeds)

entities.name: risk_intelligence_platform
entities.description: The third-party risk management platform used by Direct Travel for 24/7 global event monitoring (3.2(a)), destination-specific risk ratings covering security, health, transportation, and infrastructure dimensions (3.3(a)), and sourcing pre-trip briefing content on a continuous basis (3.3(b)).

source_anchor.source_text: using the Risk Intelligence Platform's intelligence capabilities, which aggregate data from commercially available sources with broad geographic and linguistic coverage

entities.name: specialist_provider
entities.description: Third-party providers of security, medical, evacuation, and insurance services. Where Client has contracted Specialist Providers, Direct Travel coordinates referrals and information sharing as authorized by Client. Alerts do not constitute legal, medical, or security advice unless explicitly provided by a contracted Specialist Provider.

source_anchor.source_text: Where Client has contracted Specialist Providers (security, medical, evacuation, insurance), Direct Travel will coordinate referrals and information sharing as authorized by Client

entities.name: duty_of_care_services
entities.description: The overarching set of services defined in Section 3, comprising six core components: Itinerary Visibility and Traveler Locate (3.1), Risk Intelligence and Monitoring (3.2), Pre-Trip Services (3.3), In-Trip Monitoring and Alerts (3.4), Incident Response and Traveler Assistance (3.5), and Post-Incident Reporting (3.6).

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will capture and maintain itinerary records

entities.name: itinerary_visibility_service
entities.description: Section 3.1 service component. Direct Travel captures and maintains itinerary records for Approved Channel bookings (air, hotel, rail, car rental, ground transportation), provides reporting dashboards and optionally an interactive global map, identifies Travelers in or near incident impact zones, and uses commercially reasonable efforts to ingest Off-Channel Booking data where capture tools are deployed.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will capture and maintain itinerary records for all travel booked through Approved Channels, including air, hotel, rail, car rental, and ground transportation segments

entities.name: risk_intelligence_monitoring_service
entities.description: Section 3.2 service component. Continuous 24/7 monitoring of global events using the Risk Intelligence Platform. Includes event triage and severity classification, identification of potentially impacted Travelers, initiation of notifications per Section 5, and layering active threat alerts on the global risk map. Covers six risk categories: security, natural hazards, health, transportation, political/regulatory, and infrastructure.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain continuous 24/7 monitoring of global events that may impact Travelers

entities.name: pre_trip_services
entities.description: Section 3.3 service component comprising four sub-services: Destination Risk Ratings (3.3(a)), Pre-Trip Advisories (3.3(b), SOW-configured), High-Risk Destination Approval (3.3(c), Client-configured), and Travel Insurance Verification (3.3(d), SOW-configured). Client is responsible for defining risk thresholds, approver hierarchies, and procuring insurance policies.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will make available to Client and Travelers destination-specific risk ratings sourced from the Risk Intelligence Platform

entities.name: in_trip_monitoring_service
entities.description: Section 3.4 service component. Delivers destination-specific or event-based real-time alerts to Travelers through configured channels (email, SMS, mobile app push). Alerts are informational and include event description, assessed severity, recommended actions, and Direct Travel emergency contact info. Active trip monitoring tracks events that may impact itinerary and initiates proactive outreach per Section 5 where warranted by severity.

source_anchor.source_text: Direct Travel will deliver destination-specific or event-based alerts to Travelers through one or more channels (email, SMS, mobile application push notification), as configured in the SOW

entities.name: incident_response_service
entities.description: Section 3.5 service component activated at Severity Level 2 or above. Includes welfare checks, rebooking and disruption support, Specialist Provider coordination, and Client escalation per Section 6. Subject to severity level, availability, and supplier constraints. Certain actions (evacuation, private security, medical evacuation, charter transport, premium rebooking, extraordinary costs) require express Client authorization.

source_anchor.source_text: During Incidents classified at Severity Level 2 or above, Direct Travel will provide the following services, subject to Severity Level, availability, and supplier constraints

entities.authorization_details: Express Client authorization required for: physical evacuation/extraction, private security deployment, medical evacuation/repatriation, charter/non-commercial transport, premium-class rebooking where economy alternatives exist, and actions exceeding the SOW extraordinary cost threshold. Other actions do not require authorization.

entities.name: post_incident_reporting_service
entities.description: Section 3.6 service component. Provides post-incident summary upon Client request or automatically for Level 4 Incidents, including: impacted Travelers, timeline of Direct Travel actions, outreach attempts and Traveler response statuses, supplier actions/constraints, and operational observations. For Level 4 Incidents, Direct Travel will participate in post-incident review with Client to identify program improvements and assess response effectiveness.

source_anchor.source_text: Upon Client request or automatically for Level 4 Incidents, Direct Travel will provide a post-incident summary

entities.authorization_details: Provided upon Client request for any severity; automatic for Level 4 Incidents.

entities.name: traveler_response_need_assistance
entities.description: One of four standardized welfare check response statuses. NEED ASSISTANCE indicates the Traveler requires help, triggering further TMC action and potential Specialist Provider coordination.

source_anchor.source_text: Outreach to potentially impacted Travelers to confirm status (SAFE / NEED ASSISTANCE / NOT IN AREA / NO RESPONSE)

entities.name: traveler_response_safe
entities.description: One of four standardized welfare check response statuses. SAFE indicates the Traveler has confirmed they are not in danger or need of assistance.

source_anchor.source_text: Outreach to potentially impacted Travelers to confirm status (SAFE / NEED ASSISTANCE / NOT IN AREA / NO RESPONSE)

entities.name: traveler_response_not_in_area
entities.description: One of four standardized welfare check response statuses. NOT IN AREA indicates the Traveler has confirmed they are not located in the incident impact zone.

source_anchor.source_text: Outreach to potentially impacted Travelers to confirm status (SAFE / NEED ASSISTANCE / NOT IN AREA / NO RESPONSE)

entities.name: traveler_response_no_response
entities.description: One of four standardized welfare check response statuses. NO RESPONSE indicates the Traveler has not replied to welfare check outreach, likely triggering escalation procedures.

source_anchor.source_text: Outreach to potentially impacted Travelers to confirm status (SAFE / NEED ASSISTANCE / NOT IN AREA / NO RESPONSE)

entities.name: welfare_check_workflow
entities.description: The welfare check outreach process described in Section 3.5(a) whereby Direct Travel contacts potentially impacted Travelers during Incidents at Severity Level 2 or above to confirm their status as SAFE, NEED ASSISTANCE, NOT IN AREA, or NO RESPONSE. Detailed outreach sequence and timing governed by Section 5.

source_anchor.source_text: Welfare checks — Outreach to potentially impacted Travelers to confirm status (SAFE / NEED ASSISTANCE / NOT IN AREA / NO RESPONSE)

entities.trigger_condition: Incident classified at Severity Level 2 or above with potentially impacted Travelers identified via itinerary data

entities.name: security_threats
entities.description: Risk intelligence category covering terrorism, armed conflict, crime, and civil unrest. Monitored as part of the 24/7 global event monitoring program under Section 3.2.

relationships.description: Direct Travel provides the overarching set of Duty of Care services defined in Section 3, comprising six core components: Itinerary Visibility, Risk Intelligence, Pre-Trip Services, In-Trip Monitoring, Incident Response, and Post-Incident Reporting.
Direct Travel captures and maintains itinerary records, provides reporting dashboards, an optional interactive global map, and identifies Travelers in or near incident impact zones (Section 3.1).
Direct Travel maintains continuous 24/7 monitoring of global events that may impact Travelers using the Risk Intelligence Platform (Section 3.2).
Direct Travel provides pre-trip services including destination risk ratings, pre-trip advisories, high-risk destination approval, and travel insurance verification (Section 3.3).
Direct Travel delivers destination-specific or event-based real-time alerts and active trip monitoring for the duration of a Traveler's trip (Section 3.4).
Direct Travel provides incident response and traveler assistance services during Incidents classified at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5).
Direct Travel provides post-incident summary reporting upon Client request or automatically for Level 4 Incidents, and participates in post-incident reviews (Section 3.6).
Direct Travel makes destination-specific risk ratings available to Client and Travelers, covering security, health, transportation, and infrastructure dimensions (Section 3.3(a)).

Direct Travel delivers pre-trip briefing materials to Travelers prior to departure including destination risk summary, entry requirements, emergency contacts, embassy locations, and recommended precautions (Section 3.3(b)).
Direct Travel enforces pre-trip approval workflows within the booking platform, preventing ticketing until required approval is obtained for Client-designated high-risk destinations (Section 3.3(c)).
Direct Travel verifies that Travelers have appropriate travel insurance coverage prior to departure where included in the SOW (Section 3.3(d)).
Direct Travel provides air, rail, hotel, and ground transportation rebooking, rerouting, and itinerary modification during incidents at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).
Direct Travel coordinates referrals and information sharing with Specialist Providers contracted by Client for security, medical, evacuation, or insurance services (Section 3.5(a)).
Direct Travel provides notification to Client's designated contacts per escalation protocols defined in Section 6 during Incidents at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).
Incident response and traveler assistance services are activated during Incidents classified at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).
Rebooking and disruption support is activated as part of incident response at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).
Specialist Provider coordination is activated as part of incident response at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).
Client escalation notifications are activated during Incidents at Severity Level 2 or above per Section 6 (Section 3.5(a)).
Post-incident summary reporting is automatically triggered for Level 4 Incidents, and post-incident review with Client is conducted (Section 3.6(a)-(b)).
Welfare check outreach to potentially impacted Travelers is conducted during Incidents at Severity Level 2 or above (Section 3.5(a)).

Six categories of incident response actions require express Client authorization unless otherwise agreed in the SOW: physical evacuation, private security, medical evacuation, charter transport, premium-class rebooking, and extraordinary cost actions (Section 3.5(b)).
Premium-class rebooking where economy/coach alternatives exist requires express Client authorization (Section 3.5(b)).
Direct Travel coordinates Specialist Provider referrals and information sharing as authorized by Client (Section 3.5(a)).
A NEED ASSISTANCE welfare check response indicates the Traveler requires help, triggering further TMC action and potential Specialist Provider coordination (Section 3.5(a)).
A NEED ASSISTANCE welfare check response indicates the Traveler requires help, potentially triggering rebooking and disruption support as part of incident response (Section 3.5(a)).
Travelers may respond with SAFE status during welfare check outreach, confirming they are not in danger or need of assistance (Section 3.5(a)).
Travelers may respond with NEED ASSISTANCE status during welfare check outreach, indicating they require help (Section 3.5(a)).
Travelers may respond with NOT IN AREA status during welfare check outreach, confirming they are not located in the incident impact zone (Section 3.5(a)).
Travelers may have NO RESPONSE status when they have not replied to welfare check outreach attempts (Section 3.5(a)).

24/7 global event monitoring uses the Risk Intelligence Platform's intelligence capabilities, which aggregate data from commercially available sources with broad geographic and linguistic coverage (Section 3.2(a)).
Destination-specific risk ratings are sourced from the Risk Intelligence Platform, covering security, health, transportation, and infrastructure dimensions (Section 3.3(a)).
Pre-trip briefing content is sourced from the Risk Intelligence Platform and updated on a continuous basis (Section 3.3(b)).
The itinerary visibility service provides Client access to reporting dashboards showing active Travelers by destination, travel dates, and booking status (Section 3.1(b)).
Where included in the SOW, itinerary visibility includes an interactive global map displaying Traveler locations overlaid with destination risk ratings and active threat alerts (Section 3.1(b)).
Travel booked through Approved Channels enables full itinerary capture and maintenance by Direct Travel, including air, hotel, rail, car rental, and ground transportation segments (Section 3.1(a)).
Approved Channels provide the itinerary visibility required for full Duty of Care service coverage, including locate, alert, and assistance capabilities (Section 3.1(d)).
When an Incident occurs, Direct Travel identifies Travelers who are in or near the affected impact zone using available itinerary data (Section 3.1(c)).
Incidents may be classified at Severity Level 2 or above, triggering incident response and traveler assistance services (Section 3.5(a)).
Level 4 Incidents automatically trigger post-incident summary reporting and post-incident review with Client (Section 3.6(a)-(b)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers security threats including terrorism, armed conflict, crime, and civil unrest (Section 3.2(c)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers natural hazards including earthquakes, hurricanes, flooding, wildfire, and volcanic activity (Section 3.2(c)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers health risks including disease outbreaks, pandemic developments, and environmental contamination (Section 3.2(c)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers transportation disruptions including airport closures, airline operational failures, rail strikes, and border closures (Section 3.2(c)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers political and regulatory changes including visa requirement changes, entry restrictions, and government advisories (Section 3.2(c)).
Risk intelligence monitoring covers infrastructure failures including telecommunications outages and power grid failures (Section 3.2(c)).
Client contracts Specialist Providers for security, medical, evacuation, and insurance services; Direct Travel coordinates referrals and information sharing as authorized by Client (Section 3.5(a)).
Direct Travel provides Client with access to reporting dashboards showing active Travelers by destination, travel dates, and booking status (Section 3.1(b)).

Direct Travel provides access to an interactive global map displaying Traveler locations overlaid with destination risk ratings and active threat alerts, where included in the SOW (Section 3.1(b), 3.2(b)).
Pre-trip advisory delivery is configured in the SOW; the service operates 'where configured in the SOW' (Section 3.3(b)).
Travel insurance verification is included where specified in the SOW (Section 3.3(d)).
Alert channels (email, SMS, mobile app push notification) are configured in the SOW for in-trip monitoring and alerts (Section 3.4(a)).
The extraordinary cost threshold and potential authorization overrides for incident response actions are specified in the SOW (Section 3.5(b)).
The client authorization requirement constrains six categories of incident response actions that require express Client authorization before Direct Travel may execute them (Section 3.5(b)).
Off-Channel Bookings for which itinerary data has not been provided mean Direct Travel cannot locate, alert, or assist Travelers, limiting Duty of Care service coverage (Section 3.1(d)).

Direct Travel services are not a substitute for local emergency response; Travelers remain responsible for contacting local emergency services where there is an immediate threat to life or safety (Section 3.5(c)).

Client acknowledges that Off-Channel Bookings without itinerary data are not visible to Direct Travel and accepts the resulting limitation on locate, alert, and assistance capabilities (Section 3.1(d)).
Client bears responsibility for providing express authorization for six categories of extraordinary incident response actions (Section 3.5(b)).

entities.name: Severity Level 1 — Advisory
entities.description: Minor disruption with limited direct impact on Travelers; informational alert only. Examples include localized weather warning, minor transportation delay, low-level protest in non-traveler area. Advisory alert issued within 4 hours. Client escalation not required unless Client-configured. Status updates as conditions change.

entities.name: Severity Level 2 — Monitor
entities.description: Moderate disruption that may impact Travelers; targeted alerts issued; optional welfare check. Examples include significant weather event approaching, regional transportation strike announced, elevated security alert in destination city. Targeted Traveler alert(s) within 2 hours. Client primary program contact notified within 4 hours. Status updates every 4 hours while active.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 2 | Monitor | Moderate disruption that may impact Travelers; targeted alerts issued; optional welfare check ... Targeted Traveler alert(s) within 2 hours | Notification to primary program contact within 4 hours | Every 4 hours while active

entities.name: Severity Level 3 — Action Required
entities.description: Significant disruption likely impacting one or more Travelers; proactive outreach required. Examples include major weather event (hurricane, earthquake) in Traveler area, civil unrest near Traveler locations, airport closure, disease outbreak with local transmission. Traveler outreach initiated within 60 minutes. Escalation roster notified within 60 minutes. Status updates every 2 hours while active.

source_anchor.source_text: Level 3 | Action Required | Significant disruption likely impacting one or more Travelers; proactive outreach required ... Initiate Traveler outreach within 60 minutes | Notification to escalation roster within 60 minutes | Every 2 hours while active

entities.name: Severity Level 4 — Crisis
entities.description: Immediate life safety risk or large-scale disruption affecting multiple Travelers; urgent outreach and crisis escalation. Examples include terrorist attack, active armed conflict, catastrophic natural disaster, pandemic border closures, mass-casualty transportation incident. Traveler outreach initiated within 30 minutes. Client escalation roster contacted within 30 minutes. Crisis Bridge established within 60 minutes. Status updates targeted every 30 minutes during acute phase, hourly during sustained phase.

relationships.description: Incidents may be classified as Level 1 (Advisory) — minor disruption with limited direct impact on Travelers, informational alert only.
Incidents may be classified as Level 2 (Monitor) — moderate disruption that may impact Travelers; targeted alerts issued; optional welfare check.
Incidents may be classified as Level 3 (Action Required) — significant disruption likely impacting one or more Travelers; proactive outreach required.
Incidents may be classified as Level 4 (Crisis) — immediate life safety risk or large-scale disruption affecting multiple Travelers; urgent outreach and crisis escalation.
Incidents at all severity levels may impact Travelers; severity levels are defined by degree of impact on Travelers from limited (Level 1) to immediate life safety risk (Level 4).
Traveler responds SAFE indicating they are not in immediate danger and do not require assistance.
Traveler responds NEED ASSISTANCE indicating they require support such as rebooking, information, or medical/security referral.
Traveler responds NOT IN AREA indicating they are not in or near the affected area.
Traveler has not responded within the configured outreach window, resulting in No Response status.
When Traveler indicates NEED ASSISTANCE, Direct Travel attempts live contact within 15 minutes and provides assistance per Section 3.5 (Incident Response and Traveler Assistance).
When a Traveler has not responded within the configured outreach window (No Response), escalation proceeds per Section 6.2 non-responsive traveler escalation procedure.
NEED ASSISTANCE response is followed by the obligation to attempt live contact (voice call) within 15 minutes and offer assistance consistent with Section 3.5.
At Level 2, notification is sent to the primary program contact within 4 hours per the SLO.
The primary travel program owner (primary program contact) is a Client-designated contact role who receives Level 2 escalation notifications.
Direct Travel provides incident response and traveler assistance services including rebooking, information, and medical/security referral as referenced in Section 3.5.
Direct Travel is obligated to attempt live contact (voice call) within 15 minutes when a Traveler indicates NEED ASSISTANCE.

At Level 4, the escalation workflow uses configurable parameters: 3–6 attempts (default 4) and a 30–120 minute non-response window (default 60 minutes).
During a Level 4 incident, the acute-phase SITREP cadence (default 30 min) transitions to the sustained-phase SITREP cadence (default 60 min) as the incident progresses.
The Crisis Bridge service is the core deliverable activated through the Crisis Bridge Activation Workflow for Level 4 incidents.
Parameters in Appendix A are configurable per Client and should be defined in the SOW; the Client is a party to the SOW.
Medical assistance, security response, and insurance coordination providers are Client-designated, meaning the Client engages these specialist providers.
Crisis Bridge availability (Included/Not included, default Included for Level 4) is a configurable coverage model parameter defined in the SOW.
Pre-trip service parameters (briefing delivery, risk threshold, approval workflow, insurance verification) are configurable and defined in the SOW.
Off-channel ingestion methods (email parsing, supplier direct-connect, expense integration) are configurable integration parameters defined in the SOW.
Travel insurance verification (Enabled/Disabled, default Disabled) is a configurable pre-trip parameter defined in the SOW.
Non-response outreach parameters for Level 3 (attempt count 2–5, window 60–180 min) are configurable and defined in the SOW.
Non-response outreach and coverage model parameters for Level 4 (attempt count 3–6, window 30–120 min, SITREP cadence, Crisis Bridge) are configurable and defined in the SOW.
Travel insurance verification is listed as a configurable parameter under Appendix A Section A.4 Pre-Trip Services.
The risk intelligence provider is designated by Direct Travel (currently Crisis24 Horizon) per Appendix A Section A.3, indicating Direct Travel controls this platform selection.

entities.name: direct_travel_inc
entities.description: Direct Travel, Inc., the TMC identified as the sender of all message templates in Appendix B and the point of contact for traveler assistance. Referenced across all four severity level templates as the organization travelers should contact for help, rebooking, or emergency support via its 24/7 Emergency Line.

entities.name: traveler
entities.description: The recipient of all standard message templates defined in Appendix B. All templates are addressed to travelers to inform them of events affecting their travel, request welfare status confirmation (at Level 3 and Level 4), and provide emergency contact information.

source_anchor.source_text: Based on current assessment, this event is classified as Level 1 — Advisory and is not expected to directly impact your travel plans.

entities.name: severity_level_1
entities.description: Level 1 — Advisory. As referenced in B.1, this severity classification indicates the event is not expected to directly impact travel plans. No traveler action is required; recommended action is to monitor local news and communications from Direct Travel. Only an email channel template is defined for this level.

source_anchor.source_text: Based on current assessment, this event is classified as Level 1 — Advisory and is not expected to directly impact your travel plans.

entities.name: severity_level_2
entities.description: Level 2 — Monitor. As referenced in B.2, this severity classification indicates the event may affect travel. Recommended actions include monitoring local news, ensuring mobile phone accessibility, reviewing itinerary for potential impacts, and contacting Direct Travel for rebooking assistance. SMS and email channel templates are defined for this level.

source_anchor.source_text: A [EVENT TYPE] has been reported in/near [CITY/REGION] that may affect your travel. This event is classified as Level 2 — Monitor.

entities.name: severity_level_3
entities.description: Level 3 — Action Required. As referenced in B.3, this severity classification denotes a significant event requiring traveler status confirmation via welfare check. Travelers must reply with SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA. SMS, email, and voice channel templates are defined for this level.

source_anchor.source_text: A significant [EVENT TYPE] has been reported in/near [CITY/REGION]. This event is classified as Level 3 — Action Required.

entities.name: severity_level_4
entities.description: Level 4 — Crisis. As referenced in B.4, this is the highest severity classification denoting a critical event requiring immediate traveler response. Templates use 'CRISIS ALERT' prefix and 'Immediate Response Required' subject lines. Includes statement that Direct Travel is actively coordinating response and will attempt direct contact. SMS, email, and voice channel templates are defined for this level.

source_anchor.source_text: A critical event has occurred in/near [CITY/REGION]. This is a Level 4 — Crisis alert.

entities.name: traveler_response_safe
entities.description: SAFE — a standardized welfare check response option presented in Level 3 and Level 4 message templates. Defined as 'I am safe and do not need assistance' (Level 3) or 'I am safe' (Level 4). In voice scripts, travelers can reply SAFE to the text message or press 1.

source_anchor.source_text: SAFE — I am safe and do not need assistance

entities.name: traveler_response_need_assistance

Defined as 'I need assistance (rebooking, information, or other support)' (Level 3) or 'I need immediate assistance' (Level 4). Level 3 voice script offers 'press 1 now to speak with an advisor'; Level 4 voice script offers 'press 2 or reply NEED HELP and stay on the line.'

source_anchor.source_text: NEED HELP — I need assistance (rebooking, information, or other support)

entities.name: traveler_response_not_in_area

Defined as 'I am not in or near the affected area' (Level 3) or 'I am not in the affected area' (Level 4). This option is available in SMS and email templates but is not explicitly offered in voice scripts.

source_anchor.source_text: NOT IN AREA — I am not in or near the affected area

entities.name: alert_level_1_email
entities.description: B.1 — Level 1 Advisory Alert email template. Informs traveler of event classified as Level 1 — Advisory. States event is not expected to directly impact travel plans. Recommended action: no action required, monitor local news and Direct Travel communications. Provides 24/7 emergency number for questions or concerns.

source_anchor.source_text: B.1 Level 1 — Advisory Alert (Email)

Based on current assessment, this event is classified as Level 1 — Advisory and is not expected to directly impact your travel plans. Situation: [Brief description of event]. Recommended Action: No action required at this time. Monitor local news and communications from Direct Travel for updates. If you have questions or concerns, contact Direct Travel at [24/7 EMERGENCY NUMBER] or reply to this message.

entities.name: alert_level_2_sms
entities.description: B.2 — Level 2 Monitor Alert SMS template. Brief notification informing traveler of event type reported near their city with instruction to monitor the situation and contact Direct Travel if assistance is needed.

entities.content: Travel Alert: [EVENT TYPE] reported near [CITY]. Monitor situation. Contact Direct Travel at [NUMBER] if you need assistance.

entities.name: alert_level_2_email
entities.description: B.2 — Level 2 Monitor Alert email template. Informs traveler of event classified as Level 2 — Monitor that may affect their travel. Includes recommended actions: monitor local news, ensure mobile phone is charged and accessible, review itinerary for impacts, and contact Direct Travel for rebooking assistance. States updates will be provided if conditions change.

source_anchor.source_text: Email Subject: Travel Alert — [EVENT TYPE] near [CITY/REGION] — Monitor

entities.content: Subject: Travel Alert — [EVENT TYPE] near [CITY/REGION] — Monitor. A [EVENT TYPE] has been reported in/near [CITY/REGION] that may affect your travel. This event is classified as Level 2 — Monitor. Recommended Actions: Monitor local news and official communications; Ensure your mobile phone is charged and accessible; Review your itinerary for potential impacts; Contact Direct Travel at [24/7 EMERGENCY NUMBER] if you need rebooking assistance. We will provide updates if conditions change.

entities.name: alert_level_3_sms
entities.description: B.3 — Level 3 Action Required welfare check SMS template. Uses 'TRAVEL ALERT' prefix. Requests traveler to reply with one of three standardized statuses: SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA. Instructs travelers in immediate danger to call local emergency services first.

source_anchor.source_text: SMS: TRAVEL ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY]. Are you safe? Reply: SAFE / NEED HELP / NOT IN AREA. If in immediate danger, call local emergency services first.

entities.content: TRAVEL ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY]. Are you safe? Reply: SAFE / NEED HELP / NOT IN AREA. If in immediate danger, call local emergency services first.

entities.name: alert_level_3_email
entities.description: B.3 — Level 3 Action Required welfare check email template. Uses 'URGENT' prefix in subject line. Requests traveler to confirm status by replying to email or SMS with SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA. Defines each response option: SAFE = 'I am safe and do not need assistance'; NEED HELP = 'I need assistance (rebooking, information, or other support)'; NOT IN AREA = 'I am not in or near the affected area'. Instructs travelers in immediate danger to contact local emergency services first.

source_anchor.source_text: Email Subject: URGENT: Travel Alert — [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY/REGION] — Action Required

entities.content: Subject: URGENT: Travel Alert — [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY/REGION] — Action Required. A significant [EVENT TYPE] has been reported in/near [CITY/REGION]. This event is classified as Level 3 — Action Required. Please confirm your status by replying to this email or the SMS sent to your mobile: SAFE — I am safe and do not need assistance; NEED HELP — I need assistance (rebooking, information, or other support); NOT IN AREA — I am not in or near the affected area. If you are in immediate danger, contact local emergency services first. Direct Travel 24/7 Emergency Line: [NUMBER].

entities.name: alert_level_3_voice
entities.description: B.3 — Level 3 Action Required welfare check voice script. Identifies caller as Direct Travel. Requests traveler to reply SAFE to the text message if safe, reply NEED HELP or press 1 to speak with an advisor if assistance needed. Instructs travelers in immediate danger to hang up and contact local emergency services.

source_anchor.source_text: Voice Script: "This is Direct Travel calling regarding a travel alert in [CITY/REGION]."

entities.content: This is Direct Travel calling regarding a travel alert in [CITY/REGION]. A [EVENT TYPE] has been reported. We are checking on your safety. If you are safe, please reply SAFE to the text message we sent. If you need assistance, please reply NEED HELP or press 1 now to speak with an advisor. If you are in immediate danger, hang up and contact local emergency services.

entities.name: alert_level_4_sms
entities.description: B.4 — Level 4 Crisis welfare check SMS template. Uses 'CRISIS ALERT' prefix for maximum urgency. Requests immediate reply of SAFE or NEED HELP. Instructs travelers in immediate danger to call local emergency services first. Includes Direct Travel number.

source_anchor.source_text: SMS: CRISIS ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY]. Reply SAFE or NEED HELP now.

entities.content: CRISIS ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY]. Reply SAFE or NEED HELP now. If in immediate danger, call local emergency services first. Direct Travel: [NUMBER]

entities.name: alert_level_4_email
entities.description: B.4 — Level 4 Crisis welfare check email template. Uses 'CRISIS ALERT' prefix and 'Immediate Response Required' in subject line. Requests immediate response with SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA. States Direct Travel is actively coordinating response and a team member will attempt direct contact. Urgent instruction to contact local emergency services if in immediate danger is emphasized in all caps.

source_anchor.source_text: Email Subject: CRISIS ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY/REGION] — Immediate Response Required

entities.content: Subject: CRISIS ALERT: [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY/REGION] — Immediate Response Required. A critical event has occurred in/near [CITY/REGION]. This is a Level 4 — Crisis alert. Respond immediately: SAFE — I am safe; NEED HELP — I need immediate assistance; NOT IN AREA — I am not in the affected area. IF YOU ARE IN IMMEDIATE DANGER, CONTACT LOCAL EMERGENCY SERVICES FIRST. Direct Travel is actively coordinating response. A team member will attempt to contact you directly. Direct Travel 24/7 Emergency Line: [NUMBER].

entities.name: alert_level_4_voice
entities.description: B.4 — Level 4 Crisis welfare check voice script. Identifies call as 'urgent call from Direct Travel.' Requests immediate safety confirmation: press 1 or reply SAFE if safe; press 2 or reply NEED HELP and stay on the line if help needed. Emergency services instruction is repeated twice for emphasis: 'if you are in immediate danger, hang up and call local emergency services now. I repeat — if you are in immediate danger, call local emergency services.'

source_anchor.source_text: Voice Script: "This is an urgent call from Direct Travel. A crisis event has been reported in [CITY/REGION]."

entities.content: This is an urgent call from Direct Travel. A crisis event has been reported in [CITY/REGION]. We need to confirm your safety immediately. If you are safe, press 1 or reply SAFE to our text message. If you need help, press 2 or reply NEED HELP and stay on the line. If you are in immediate danger, hang up and call local emergency services now. I repeat — if you are in immediate danger, call local emergency services.

entities.name: welfare_check_workflow
entities.description: The multi-step outreach workflow for contacting travelers during incidents. Appendix B provides the standardized message templates used in this workflow: Level 3 (Action Required) and Level 4 (Crisis) templates explicitly implement welfare checks across SMS, email, and voice channels, requesting travelers to confirm their status as SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA.

source_anchor.source_text: B.3 Level 3 — Action Required Welfare Check (SMS + Email + Voice)

relationships.description: The Level 1 Advisory email template is addressed and sent to the traveler, informing them of an event classified as Level 1.
The Level 2 Monitor SMS template is sent to the traveler, notifying them of a reported event near their city with instructions to monitor the situation.
The Level 2 Monitor email template is sent to the traveler, informing them of an event classified as Level 2 that may affect their travel.
The Level 3 Action Required welfare check SMS is sent to the traveler requesting a status reply of SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA.
The Level 3 Action Required welfare check email is sent to the traveler requesting status confirmation by replying with SAFE, NEED HELP, or NOT IN AREA.
The Level 3 Action Required voice call is made to the traveler, identifying itself as Direct Travel and requesting safety confirmation.
The Level 4 Crisis welfare check SMS with CRISIS ALERT prefix is sent to the traveler requesting immediate reply of SAFE or NEED HELP.
The Level 4 Crisis welfare check email with 'Immediate Response Required' subject line is sent to the traveler requesting immediate status response.
The Level 4 Crisis urgent voice call is made to the traveler requesting immediate safety confirmation via keypress or text reply.
The B.1 email template is used when an event is classified as Level 1 — Advisory, as stated: 'this event is classified as Level 1 — Advisory.'
The B.2 SMS template is used when an event is classified as Level 2 — Monitor.
The B.2 email template is used when an event is classified as Level 2 — Monitor, as stated: 'This event is classified as Level 2 — Monitor.'
The B.3 SMS template is used when an event is classified as Level 3 — Action Required.
The B.3 email template is used when an event is classified as Level 3 — Action Required, as stated: 'This event is classified as Level 3 — Action Required.'
The B.3 voice script is used for Level 3 — Action Required welfare checks, delivered as part of the Level 3 multi-channel outreach.
The B.4 SMS template is used when an event is classified as Level 4 — Crisis, using the CRISIS ALERT prefix.
The B.4 email template is used when an event is classified as Level 4 — Crisis, as stated: 'This is a Level 4 — Crisis alert.'
The B.4 voice script is used for Level 4 — Crisis welfare checks, identified as 'an urgent call from Direct Travel' regarding a crisis event.
Travelers respond with SAFE status in Level 3 and Level 4 welfare checks, meaning 'I am safe and do not need assistance' (Level 3) or 'I am safe' (Level 4).

Travelers respond with NEED HELP status in Level 3 and Level 4 welfare checks, meaning 'I need assistance (rebooking, information, or other support)' (Level 3) or 'I need immediate assistance' (Level 4).
Travelers respond with NOT IN AREA status in Level 3 and Level 4 welfare checks, meaning 'I am not in or near the affected area.'
The Level 3 welfare check SMS is the first channel step in the Level 3 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The Level 3 welfare check email is a channel step in the Level 3 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The Level 3 welfare check voice call is a channel step in the Level 3 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The Level 4 crisis welfare check SMS is the first channel step in the Level 4 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The Level 4 crisis welfare check email is a channel step in the Level 4 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The Level 4 crisis welfare check voice call is a channel step in the Level 4 multi-channel welfare check outreach (SMS + Email + Voice).
The welfare check workflow with traveler status confirmation is activated at Level 3 — Action Required, as B.3 templates request travelers to confirm their status.
The welfare check workflow with traveler status confirmation is activated at Level 4 — Crisis, as B.4 templates request immediate traveler response.
In the Level 3 multi-channel outreach (SMS + Email + Voice), SMS is sent first, followed by email. The voice script references 'the text message we sent,' confirming SMS precedes voice.
In the Level 3 multi-channel outreach (SMS + Email + Voice), the email is followed by the voice call as the third channel in the sequence.
In the Level 4 multi-channel outreach (SMS + Email + Voice), SMS is sent first, followed by email.
In the Level 4 multi-channel outreach (SMS + Email + Voice), the email is followed by the voice call as the third channel in the sequence.

entities.name: Incident Activity Log
entities.description: A structured log template maintained by Direct Travel during Level 3 and Level 4 Incidents. Contains 14 defined fields: Incident ID, Incident Classification (Severity Level 1–4 and event type), Date/Time Opened (UTC), Date/Time Closed (UTC), Event Description, Affected Geography, Impacted Travelers (count and list), Outreach Log (per-traveler timestamp, channel, attempt number, outcome), Traveler Status Summary (counts by SAFE, NEED ASSISTANCE, NOT IN AREA, No Response), Client Escalation Log (timestamps and contacts reached on Client escalation roster), Actions Taken (chronological TMC actions including rebookings, referrals, coordination), Specialist Provider Engagement (provider name, time engaged, services requested), Crisis Bridge Log (Level 4 only — activation time, participants, SITREP timestamps), and Resolution Notes (closure summary and follow-up actions).

entities.name: Obligation to Maintain Incident Activity Log

The log must contain all 14 specified fields covering incident identification, traveler impact, outreach tracking, escalation records, actions taken, specialist provider engagement, Crisis Bridge details (Level 4), and resolution notes.

relationships.description: Direct Travel maintains the Incident Activity Log during Level 3 and Level 4 Incidents, recording all 14 defined fields.

The Impacted Travelers field records the count and list of identified Travelers within the incident impact zone.
SAFE is one of four traveler response statuses tracked in the Traveler Status Summary field of the incident activity log.
NEED ASSISTANCE is one of four traveler response statuses tracked in the Traveler Status Summary field of the incident activity log.
NOT IN AREA is one of four traveler response statuses tracked in the Traveler Status Summary field of the incident activity log.
No Response is one of four traveler response statuses tracked in the Traveler Status Summary field of the incident activity log.
